test.name: Write a proved-tier proof for the completed parentage research
test.description: The flynn-resolved scenario has all planned searches completed including a negative probate result. The skill should produce a `proved` tier proof — research is now reasonably exhaustive (per the populated stop_criteria in q_001) and three independent original sources converge on the conclusion. Tests the high-confidence tier path, distinct from the `probable` tier the seed test covers.

input.user_message: Write the final proof conclusion for Patrick Flynn's parentage.

additional_criteria: Should produce `tier: "proved"` — q_001's `exhaustive_declaration` is populated with stop_criteria, three independent sources converge, the negative probate is incorporated as part of the exhaustive search (not as a missing piece)

Should explicitly address why `proved` is justified: three independent original sources (1850 census co-residence, 1860 explicit relationship, 1908 death certificate naming Thomas as father), negative probate doesn't contradict, no unresolved conflicts remain (c_001 is resolved)
Should incorporate the negative probate result (log_006) into the narrative — exhaustive search includes documenting what was searched and didn't turn up, not just what did
Should cite the resolved birthplace conflict c_001 within the narrative — proof conclusions don't omit resolved conflicts; they're part of the GPS audit trail showing the analytical work

`narrative_markdown` should be a standalone, FamilySearch-uploadable proof document with inline citations to all four sources (src_001 through src_004) — a reader with no access to research.json should be able to follow it
